payload.content: REVISIT: APP-ADR-034 should be superseded. User has requested Z3 integration multiple times. The pure-Go propositional approach has known limitations: 19% unparseable semi-formals (arithmetic, higher-order), 30-40% recall ceiling for semantic contradictions. Z3 via subprocess (not CGo binding) would preserve single-binary while gaining SMT power. New ADR needed: APP-ADR-038 Z3 subprocess integration for Tier 5 consistency.

Full Convergence audit identified 10 gaps between spec prose and implementation reality. Phase 1: code-bridge.md claims 2 or 4 consistency tiers but implementation has 6. APP-INV-020 violation scenario says events stored in SQLite but reality is JSONL. 7 tier numbering corrections + 1 violation scenario fix needed.

PHASE 1 GAPS (spec text contradicts implementation):
Gap A1: code-bridge.md line ~367 says 'Four detection tiers' — should be Six. Line ~623 says 'two tiers. Tier 1 (graph-based)...Tier 2 (Z3-based)' — should be 'five tiers (Tiers 2-6)'. Lines 625,639 label as 'Tier 1' what is actually Tier 2. Line 653 labels Tier 5 as 'Tier 2'.
Gap A2: APP-INV-020 violation scenario at line ~157 says 'event stream is stored in the same SQLite file' — implementation uses JSONL files since Phase 5D.
These are spec-only changes. Zero code modifications. Must use ddis patch.

Phase 2: Tiers 3, 4, 6 have no implementation chapters in code-bridge.md. The consistency architecture section documents Tier 2 (graph) and Tier 5 (SMT/Z3) but omits Tier 3 (SAT/gophersat), Tier 4 (heuristic+semantic), and Tier 6 (LLM-as-judge). An agent reading the spec gets incomplete information about how three of six consistency tiers operate.

Phase 3: Event Stream Completeness. APP-INV-053 semi-formal lists only {parse, validate, drift, contradict, patch} but 8 additional state-mutating commands (render, skeleton, manifestsync, state, absorb, refine, init, witness) do not emit events. Need to: (1) update semi-formal in lifecycle-ops.md, (2) wire emitEvent calls into 8 CLI commands.

Phase 4: D1 — mode classification result (from ClassifyMode) is computed but never consumed by navigation commands. D2 — refine judge has no convergence detection. Need spec sections for mode-influenced navigation and convergence criteria, plus code changes to next.go, judge.go, and plan.go.

Phase 5: ddis next with no workspace should suggest ddis init, not ddis parse. Currently next.go detects missing DB and suggests parse, but doesn't check whether manifest.yaml or .ddis/ directory exists.

Beginning 11-issue remediation campaign. Formal framework: (1) ClearSpecByPath must be a natural transformation preserving the witness-challenge adjunction — save/restore challenge_results alongside witnesses. (2) Check 3 orphan detection must respect the section tree's partial order — if parent is reachable, children inherit reachability. (3) Check 4 glossary counting must be idempotent over nested content — count only leaf sections. (4) Bold-term extraction needs semantic filtering — exclude structural patterns (field labels, headings). Issues: #4(P0), #6-#15(P1-P3). All code fixes in ddis-cli, all spec fixes via ddis CLI.

Remediation progress: Issue #4 (P0) FIXED — ClearSpecByPath now preserves challenge results alongside witnesses, 55/55 survive re-parse. Issue #6 (P1) FIXED — Check 3 now propagates reachability through parent-child tree (382→42 orphans). Issue #7 (P1) FIXED — Check 4 counts only leaf sections, eliminated double-counting. Issues #9,#10 (P2) FIXED — case-insensitive glossary matching + semantic bold-term filtering. Issue #11 (P2) FIXED — expanded exempt list for structural sections. Issue #8 (P1) FIXED — APP-INV-048/049 constitution descriptions unswapped, ownership added to frontmatter+manifest. Issue #12 FIXED — 3 witnesses upgraded from annotation to test. Issue #14 DONE — 2 glossary terms added. Overall: 610→61 warnings (90% reduction), Check 17 now PASSES.
